---
title: Preregistration
---

## What is preregistration?

* It is the act of specifying in advance how data will be collected and analysed.
* Preregistrations are time-stamped, immutable documents.
* Preregistrations make the distinction between hypothesis testing, conﬁrmatory research and hypothesis generating, exploratory research more clear.
* Presenting exploratory results as conﬁrmatory increases the publish ability of results at the expense of credibility of results.

## How to preregister?

The [Preregistration Challenge](https://www.cos.io/initiatives/prereg-more-information) was an education campaign designed to introduce preregistration as a regular habit. It includes a guided workﬂow to help you create a preregistration.

See the [COS Preregistration page](https://www.cos.io/initiatives/prereg) for more information about preregistration and start working on your own preregistration in [OSF Registries](https://osf.io/registries).

## Resources and Further Readings

* *Can you preregister if you are using existing data?* Yes, see template available at [osf.io/registries](https://osf.io/registries)
* ["The Preregistration Revolution"](https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1708274114), which covers nine challenging situations in which preregistration is beneﬁcial
* [A blog series about preregistration](https://www.cos.io/blog/tag/preregistration)
* The Guardian: [Trust in science would be improved by study pre-registration](https://www.theguardian.com/science/blog/2013/jun/05/trust-in-science-study-pre-registration)
* Nature: [A manifesto for reproducible science](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41562-016-0021)
* A [presentation](https://osf.io/dh62a/) from a community member introducing OSF
* A helpful [OSF presentation](https://osf.io/zxmkf/) on how to create your first works
